ety: for shū = Zend fshu = S. पशु+bān, q.v.
def: s.m. A shepherd; a cowherd.
more: s. m. A cowherd or shepherd.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian چوپَان (čōpān).

def: s.f. The office of shepherd or cowherd.

ety: Prk. चोप्पडो; prob. fr. S. च्यु(च्यवते)
def: s.m. Oil, grease, butter (see ćupaṛnā).
more: s. f. The name of a game played with oblong dice; the cloth on which it is played. See چوپاڙ.

ety: ćupar˚ = Prk. चोप्पड(इ); prob. fr. S. च्यावय(ति), caus. fr. rt. च्यु

v.t. = ćupaṛnd, q.v.
v.t. = ćupaṛnd, q.v.

def: adj. Oiled, oily, greasy, &c. ( = ćupṛā)
v.t. (local) = ćupaṛnā, q.v.
v.n. To move quietly or stealthily; to keep perfectly quiet or silent.
adv. Perfectly silent, quite still, silently, &c. = ćup-ćāp, q.v.s.v. ćup.
adj. (f. -ī), Silent, quiet, mute; secret, stealthy, sly; — ćupke, adv. Silently, quietly, with little noise; secretly, stealthily, clandestinely, furtively, slyly
subs: adv. Quietly, &c. ( = ćupke); in a low voice, in a whisper

def: s.f. Silence ( = ćup)
subs: (-ko), To have a fit of silence; to be struck dumb.

def: s.f. Silence, taciturnity (cf. ćup, and ćupkī).

s.f. A cake rubbed over with, or dipped in, clarified butter.

subs: ćur-ćūr, adj. Broken to atoms, utterly crushed, shivered; — ćur-ćār, s.m. Fragments; crumbs
v.t. To break into small pieces, to shatter; to reduce to powder
v.n. To be broken into small pieces, to be shivered; to be reduced to powder; to be exhausted
To be enamoured (of), to dote; to be steeped (in liquor), be intoxicated, be besotted
wety: Inherited from Old Hindi چور (cvr /⁠cor⁠/) (c. 1392), from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀘𑁄𑀭 (cora), from Sanskrit चोर (corá), चौर (caurá). Compare Romani chor.

def: s.m. The ceremony of tonsure; a top-knot; a crest; a diadem; a ring, an amulet; the bracelet put on a bride at her marriage; the gold or silver ornament worn by Hindū widows; a ring put on the end of an elephant's tusk (cf. ćūṛā, ćūṛī).
more: s. m. 1. A silver or gold ornament worn by the Hindū widows. 2. The ring fastened to elephantsʼ teeth.

def: s.m. Powder; filings; sawdust; bruised grain (to be chucked into the mouth); a small piece, a fragment
more: n. m. 1. Powder ( chūr ); bruised grain. 2. Sawdust ( burādā ). chūrā k. , v. a. To bruise; powder.
n. m. The funeral pile on which Sattī is performed.

adj. 1. Broad; wide; expanded. 2. Destroyed; abandoned (tenement, etc). Āge dauṛ, pīchhe chauṛ . Prov. Who runs before leaves behind. (He learns the new, forgets the old). chauṛā h. , v. n. 1. To be wide. 2. To be undone, ruined, bankrupt.
n. m. An open field or plain far from human habitation.
n. m. Rice in the husk, boiled and then slightly parched and pounded. chūṛā bhanḍār , n. m. An allowance for the maintenance of the junior members of a zamindar's family.
n. m. Bangles made of lac, worn by single women and femes coverts. Ek kauṛiyā gāṅṭhī, chūṛā penhūṅ ki māṭhī? E. Prov. One kauri's all she's got to spare, 'shall I buy bangles round or square?'
n. m. A top-knot. See choṭī. chūṛā-manī , n. m. An ornament worn by women on the top-knot.
s. m. 1. See چبوتره. 2. The place where Hindū women are burned.
s. m. 1. Filings. 2. Sawdust. 3. Grain reduced to coarse particles to be chucked into the mouth. 4. (dakh.) A particle or small portion, atom, scrap. چوراکرنا chūrā karnā, To break to atoms.
subs: v.t. To break to atoms; to reduce to powder; to bruise, to pulverize

def: s.m. = ćūrā, q.v. s.m. Powder; filings; sawdust; bruised grain (to be chucked into the mouth); a small piece, a fragment
more: s. m. (dakh.) Piece, bit: see ريزه reza.
subs: v.t. To break to atoms; to reduce to powder; to bruise, to pulverize

def: s.f. The ceremony of tonsure; a single lock or tuft of hair left on the crown of the head after the ceremony of tonsure; a top-knot; crest (of a bird, &c.); plume; diadem; head, top, summit
more: adj. Wide, broad. چوڙا چکلا chauṛā-chaklā, adj. Extensive, spacious.
s. m. 1. ( चूडामणि ) The rings fastened to elephantsʼ teeth. 2. A kind of ornament. 3. ( चिपिट ) A kind of food made of parched rice.
subs: s.m. Shaving the head all but one lock, tonsure
s.m. An ornament or jewel worn on the top of the head; a jewel worn in a crest or diadem.

def: s.m. A top-knot ( = jūṛā); a crest, plume, diadem ( = ćoṭī); an ornament worn on the top of the head; a ring (of brass, &c.) put on the extremity of an elephant's tusk; a ring or bangle of lac (worn by single women and femmes couvertes).

ety: S. चूर्णकः or चिपिटः; cf. ćyūṛā
def: s.m. Rice boiled in the husk, and then parched and pounded
subs: ćūṛā-bhanḍār, s.m. An allowance for the maintenance of the junior members of a zamīndār's family.

def: s.f. The ghost of a woman who dies in a state of pregnancy or of impurity; a witch, hag, beldam; a fury, a shrew; a dirty or ugly old woman, a slut, slattern.
more: s. f. 1. The ghost of a woman who died while pregnant. 2. A hag, a fury. 3. A dirty woman, slut, slattern.
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit *𑀘𑀼𑀟 (*cuḍa) + Middle Indo-Aryan -𑀇𑀮𑁆𑀮- (-illa-). Cognate with Gujarati ચૂડેલ (cūḍel). Related to چُوت (cūt, “cunt”)، چُڑ (cuṛ, “vulva”)، چُڈّو (cuḍḍo, “harlot”).

def: adj. & s.m. Having a single lock of hair on the crown of the head; having a top-knot; crested; tufted; — a crested one, &c.; — ćūṛālā, s.m. A kind of cyperus, Kyllinga monocephala.
adj. & s.m. Having a single lock of hair on the crown of the head; having a top-knot; crested; tufted; — a crested one, &c.; — ćūṛālā, s.m. A kind of cyperus, Kyllinga monocephala.
adj. & s.m. = ćūrāman, and ćūṛā-maṇī, qq.v.
s.m. Stealing, thieving, robbing.
s.m. = ćūran, q.v. s.m. Stealing, thieving, robbing.

def: v.t. To steal, filch, pilfer, rob; to plagiarize; to misappropriate; to suck in or up, to absorb; to draw back; to turn aside or away (the eyes)
subs: To take a furtive glance (at), not to look straight at (one).

def: s.f.(m.?), A wrinkle (in the skin); a crease (in cloth); — (dialec.) envy, jealousy; enmity, spite, grudge, rancour.
adj. = ćūṛāl, q.v. adj. & s.m. Having a single lock of hair on the crown of the head; having a top-knot; crested; tufted; — a crested one, &c.; — ćūṛālā, s.m. A kind of cyperus, Kyllinga monocephala.

ety: S. चूर्ण or H. चूरा redupl.
def: adj. (f. -ī), Crisp, short (like pie-crust), crumbling.
s.f. Dregs, lees, sediment ( = ćurrī).
s.f. A small well.
s.f. A bangle or ring made of lac or glass, &c. ( = ćūṛī, q.v.)
subs: s.m. A maker, or a vendor, of glass or lac bangles.

def: s.f. = ćurī, ćūṛī, q.v.
subs: s.m. See s.v. ćurī. s.f. A small well.

def: s.f. A cake of bread rich with ghī.
more: n. f. S. चौर्य Pr. choriam ; Pālī. chorikā . 1. Stealing; theft; embezzlement. Chorī aur sir-sori! Prov. What! steal and show fight! 2. Hiding; concealment; stealth. Chandā to he utār lūṅ lambe bāṅs maṅgāë, Maiṅ gaī pī kī choriyāṅ, taiṅ ne daī batāë . Dohā. O treacherous moon! I'll get a bamboo tall and bring you down, I to my lover hiding stole, you have my secret blown. chorī jārī, chorī chhinalā , n. f. Theft and adultery. Lākhan mānas kar dīye ūjaṛ, purkhā, chorī jārī ne . Rus. chorī chorī, chorī se, chorī chhipe se ; Sant. hape hape te , adv. Secretly; furtively; clandestinely; on the sly; in a hole and corner. chorī khafīf . Small or petty theft. chorī kā māl , n. m. Stolen property. chorī k. , v. n. To steal ( churānā ). chorī lagānā , v. a. To charge or accuse of theft. chorī maë wārdāt-i-saṅgīn . Aggravated theft. ḍhor chorī , Cattle lifting or stealing.

n. f. The broken grain which falls out of a mill. Chunye khudye posloṅ dhīyā, Aël damda, le gail dhīyā . Mag. With scraps of broken bread picked up a daughter reared, A husband came and straight my daughter disappeared.

n. f. pl. chūṛiyāṅ . 1. Bangles made of lac or glass. Tum kar doge to kyā tumhārī chūṛiyāṅ phūṭ jāëṅgīṅ? Will your bangles break if you do it? (A taunt addressed by a woman to a man). 2. Gather; pucker. 3. A ring. 4. The ring of an eye glass. chūṛiyāṅ pahannā , v. n. lit . to put on bangles. 1. To marry again. Devar par chūṛiyāṅ pahan līṅ . For her younger brother-in-law she put on her bangles again. 2. To be effeminate. chūṛiyāṅ pahnānā, oṛhnā uṛhānā, pallā ghālnā , v. a. To marry a widow. Rus. chūṛiyāṅ ṭhanḍī k. , v. a. To break one's bangles. It is the custom for women to break their lac bangles on the death of the husband. Hence, a superstitious dread of the word toṛnā , and the substitution in its place of the phrase ṭhaṅḍī k ., to cool, put out. Gar kahegī mujh se kuchh, mūṅh phoṛ-kar, bājī, tū phir, Ṭhaṅdī kar ḍālūṅgī, maiṅ hātoṅ kī sārī chūṛiyāṅ . Wom. Rangīn. If, sister shameles…
s. f. Theft, robbery, stealth. چوري چوري chorī-chorī, adv. By stealth. clandestinely.
s. f. 1. A bread rich with ghī. 2. ( S̱. see چوڙي) Bracelet.
s. Bracelet, (dakh.) ring, bangles: see چوڙي chūṛī.
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀘𑁄𑀭𑀻 (corī), from Sanskrit चौरिका (caurikā).

def: s.f. = ćūnī; and ćūṛī, qq.v. s.f. Bruised or broken grain, pulse coarsely split or ground; — a spark, a small ruby or other gem ( = ćunnī).
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀘𑁄𑀭𑀻 (corī), from Sanskrit चौरिका (caurikā).

def: s.f. (dim. of ćūṛā). A ring or bangle of lac or glass (worn on the wrist); a ring; ring (of an eye-glass); a gather, pucker
more: s. f. 1. ( चूडामणि ) Bangles or rings made of glass, &c. and worn on the wrist. See چوري chūrī, sign. 2. چوڙي دار chūṛī-dār, Drawers, or sleeves, made too long, so as to be crumpled into plaits. 2. (dakh.) See ريزه reza.
subs: adj. & s.m. Gathered, puckered; — drawers or sleeves made too long, so as to be crumpled into plaits
s.m. A maker of glass or lac bangles
v.n. 'To put on bangles'; to marry again; — to be effeminate
To break (one's) bangles (as a woman does on the death of her husband; — the verb ṭhanḍī karnā being used superstitiously for toṛnā).
wety: Feminine of چوڑا (cūṛā), from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀘𑀽𑀟 (cūḍa), from Sanskrit चूड (cūḍa).

def: s.m. Striped cloth.
s.f. A lock of hair; a ringlet, curl.
s.m. Powder.
s.m. A kind of sweatmeat made of crumbled bread or bread-crumbs (ćūr) mixed with butter and sugar.
more: Mah. n. m. A sweetmeat made of sugar, butter, and bread crumbs.
s. m. A kind of sweetmeat made of sugar and crumbled bread.

def: adj. Crushed, reduced to powder, pulverized (also written ćūrmūr; cf. mūrā-meṭ).
more: adj. Pounded; pulverized; reduced to powder.

def: adj. = ćur-mur, q.v. adj. Crushed, reduced to powder, pulverized (also written ćūrmūr; cf. mūrā-meṭ).
s.f. = H چرمرا चुरमुरा ćur-murā, s.m. (onomat.) An imitative sound; murmuring, muttering, fretting, repining, &c.
s.m. Cleaned rice, soaked, flattened, and parched (used as ćubenā).
s.m. = H چرمر चुरमुर ćur-mur, s.f. (onomat.) An imitative sound; murmuring, muttering, fretting, repining, &c.
v.n. To murmur, mutter, fret, moan (over); to be grieved, be vexed (internally).
more: v. n. To smart; burn. chirmirā'haṭ , n. f. A smart; burn; a prickly sensation.

def: v.t. To crush, reduce to small pieces or atoms, to reduce to powder.
more: v. a. To reduce to very small pieces, or to atoms. Roṭī chūr do .
v. a. To reduce to very small pieces or to atoms.

def: v.n. To be stolen ( = ćorī honā).

s.m. Powder, any minute particle of substance; flour; dust; aromatic powder, pulvil, pounded sandal, &c.; a digestive powder (composed of ground spices, dried green mangoes, limes, &c.); fossil alkali, efflorescent salt
more: n. m. Pālī, chuṅṅakam . A digestive made of powdered spices, dried green mangoes, limes, etc. 1. Merā chūran haigā ṭhos, buḍḍhā khāë samhāle hos! Street cry. I have a rich digestive rare, old men it sets up tight and square. 2. Chūran hai albelā, jisko khāveṅ gabrū chhailā! Street cry. Digestive rare ate by young men and wantons gay! 3. Aur jo kam khāëṅ to phir zof ze gash ātā hai, Pet bhar khāëṅ to phir chāhiye chūran ko ṭakā . Nazīr. Who eat not food enough through weakness faint away, Who eat a bellyful two pice for tonic pay.
s. m. A powder composed of medicines for promoting digestion.
subs: s.m. A pebble, gravel, hardened fragment of earth or brick; limestone nodule (com. called kaṅkar)

def: v.n. To be troubled with ascarides; to be irritable or peevish; to take ill or amiss.
more: Rus. n. m. S. चर to eat. Ascarides. churne lagnā , v. n. To take ill or amiss.

def: s.f. Fried or pounded rice, or grain of any kind (eaten dry by chucking into the mouth).
s.f. Dregs, sediment ( = ćurćurī, q.v.). s.f. Dregs, lees, sediment ( = ćurrī).
adj. Wrinkled; creased; — (dialec.) envious, jealous; ill-disposed, mimical, spiteful, malicious, rancorous.
s.m. Rice partially boiled in the husk, and then flattened and parched.
more: s. m. Rice prepared by soaking and toasting in the husk ( Qanoon-e-islam, glossary, p. lxiv): See چڙوا chuṛūʼā.

def: s.f. = ćuruk, q.v. s.f. Chirping (of birds), chirp, chirrup.
v.n. = ćuraknā, q.v.
more: v. n. To gabble, to prattle.

def: s.f. Chirping (of birds), chirp, chirrup.
v.n. To chirp, chirrup; to prattle, babble, gabble.
v.t. To cause to steal; to cause to be stolen, get (a thing) stolen.
s.m. One who is in the habit of sucking; (fig.), a tippler.
v.t. To cause to suck, to give to suck.
more: (caus. of چوسنا) v. a. To cause or give to suck.

def: s.f. A draught or a mouthful of water or other liquid; a suck, a sip; a pull (at a huqqa or pipe)
more: n. f. S. चूष to suck. A draught of water, etc.; a pull at a pipe. chuskī lagānā , v. a. To sip.
s. f. A mouthful of drink.
subs: To take a suck or sip, to sip.

ety: Prk. चूसणअं; S. चूषणीयं rt. चूष्
def: v.t. To suck; to absorb, drink in, draw in; to appropriate; to kiss; to exhaust
more: v. a. S. चुष 1. To suck; absorb; drink in; draw. 2. To exhaust; take it out of one.
subs: v.t. (intens.) To suck dry; to absorb altogether, &c.
wety: Inherited from Sanskrit चूषति (cūṣati).

def: v.n. To be sucked, to be absorbed.
s.f. A sucking stick (for children), a (child's) coral; a sucking-bottle ( = ćūsnī).
more: n. f. A stick for a child to suck; a child's coral; a sucking bottle.

more: s. f. Childʼs coral, a sucking-stick for children.

def: s.f. The juice of fruits; anything to be sucked or sipped.
adj. Quick, brisk, active, fleet; smart, adroit, expert, clever, ingenious; narrow, strait, tight, close; compact, well set; fit, suiting; elegant, beautiful; strong, forcible, terse
more: adj. 1. Tight; close; compact; smart; well-set. 2. Active; adroit; clever ( chālāk ). Gavāh chust, muddaī sust . Prov. The claimant backward, the witness forward. 3. Forcible; vigorous; lively. Yeh tahrīr baṛī chust hai .
adj. 1. Active, fleet, ingenious. 2. Narrow, strait, tight. چست چالاک chust-chālāk, adj. Active, alert, fleet, agile.
subs: ćust-o-ćālāk, adj. Active, alert, fleet, agile.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian چُسْت (čust).

def: adj. & s.m. = ćusta, q.v.
more: (from چست) adj. Tight, fitting well (as, clothes).

def: adj. & s.m. Tight, fitting well (as clothes); — a wrinkle, a crease; — the ventricle of a sheep, or goat, or calf; rennet; tripe; the rectum.
s.f. Agility, alertness, activity, fleetness, nimbleness; speed, expedition, despatch; smartness, dexterity, skill, adroitness, address.
more: n. f. 1. Alertness; nimbleness; activity; expedition; despatch. 2. Adroitness; address ( chālākī , 1).
s. f. Activity, alertness, fleetness, nimbleness.

def: s.m. The mango tree, Mangifera indica; — the anus.
more: n. f. S. च्युति Pudendum muliebre .

n. f. S. चुट to cut off. 1. A hurt; bruise; personal injury; wound; damage. 2. A stroke; blow. 3. A turn ( bārī ; 1). Pahl choṭ usne kīyā . 4. Rivalry; competition. Choṭ-wāle se choṭ hai. choṭ par choṭ . 1. Blow on blow; stroke on stroke. 2. One misfortune after another. choṭ k. , v. a. 1. To hurt; sting; bite; wound. Sāṅp aur chor dabe par choṭ kartā hai . Prov. A snake and a thief will not hurt you till you hurt them. 2. To cast a spell or charm; bewitch. Chitvan meṅ jādū bharā kare lākh meṅ choṭ; Kitne hī ghāyal huë, kitne hī gaë loṭ. choṭ khānā, yā lagnā , v. n. 1. To be wounded or hurt; to receive a blow. 2. To suffer loss or damage ( ṭakkar khānā ). Ranḍī kī gālī, aur bhūt ke pattharoṅ kī choṭ nahīṅ lagtī . Prov. A woman's abuse and a stone thrown by a devil dont hurt any body.
n. m. H. choṭī top-knot. A blanket or sheet worn so as to cover the head and shoulders ( ghugghī ).
s. f. Vulva. چوت مراني chūt-marānī, Strumpet.
wety: Inherited from Ashokan Prakrit *𑀘𑀼𑀢𑁆𑀢 (*cutta).

def: s.f. Vulva
subs: s.f. Fees paid by a bridegroom to the Qāẓī on the morning after his nuptials
s.f. A strumpet; a term of abuse.
wety: Inherited from Ashokan Prakrit *𑀘𑀼𑀢𑁆𑀢 (*cutta).

def: s.m. A crack, a snap, a noise.
more: s. f. 1. A hurt, a blow, bruise, damage. 2. Fall. 3. Spite, effort, attempt, assiduity, desire, wish, aim. چوٿ پر چوٿ choṭ par choṭ, One misfortune follows another, misfortunes come not singly. چوٿ باندهنا choṭ bāndhnā, 1. To defend. 2. To bind up the edge of a sword, &c. 3. To restrain by magick. چوٿ کهانا choṭ khānā, 1. To be hurt, to receive a blow. 2. To suffer loss.
s. m. A crack, a noise.
wety: Inherited from Ashokan Prakrit *𑀘𑁄𑀝𑁆𑀝 (*coṭṭa), ultimately from Sanskrit चुट् (cuṭ, “to cut”).

ety: Prk. चुडक्क(इ) or चुडक्के(इ), fr. S. चुट+कृ
def: v.t. To nip, to bite; to pluck (a flower)
v.t. To wound, to hurt; to attack ( = ćoṭ karnā).
v.t. = ćuṭālnā, q.v. v.t. To wound, to hurt; to attack ( = ćoṭ karnā).

more: n. m. S. स्फिचौ, H. chūt anus. The back-side; bottom; posteriors; bum; buttock; rump. 1. Chūtaṛ bhāṅg lāgī hai . Boys' rhyme. 2. Khāyā aur chūtaroṅ se hāth poṅchh līye . 3. Chūtaṛ jhāṛ-ke chal dīyā. chūtaṛ bajānā , v. n. To slap one's rumps with joy. chūtaṛ pīṭnā ; Sant. ḍeke piṭao , v. n. lit . To beat one's buttocks. 1. To lament; grieve over; weep for. Phr. To beat one's breasts; to wring one's hands. Chhan pakāī! chhan pakāī! chhan pakke kī morī, Sāsū susre chūtaṛ pīṭeṅ, 'hāë kachaurī thoṛī !' Chhan. 'I've cooked a verse! I've cooked a verse! my verse I give to you: Their bums beat mother and father-in-law, dāl cakes, alas! too few.' 2. To be overjoyed, cock-a-whoop. chūtaṛ dikhānā, chūtṛoṅ pe khānā , v. n. To turn tail ( pīṭh dikhānā ). Tum hameshā chūtaṛoṅ par khāte ho! Your wounds are all behind! chūtaṛ se kān gāṅṭhnā, chūtṛoṅ supārī toṛnā ( Vulg .) v. n. ( Ironic ). To do wonders…

def: n. m. H. ḍūbnā to be drowned. 1. Low ground where water lies; swamp; marsh; lowlands. 1. Ḍābar ḍūbe jag tire, jag ḍūbe dābar tire . Prov. Low lands submerged, a world's rich freight rides on the main. The world drought struck, the low lands flood the world with grain. 2. Ḍābar māṅ dhān buāūṅgī; jib ubjeṅge dhān ghanere Din din chāval khāūṅgi . Rus. Wom song. In low land rice I'll set, a rich crop I shall get, Then rice each day I'll eat 2. A small tank; a pond; a hollow in which water lies. 3. A waterpot. ḍābar-nainī , adj. 1. Having large eyes. 2. Ready to shed tears. kachhuā dābar , adj. Uneven; rough. Kachhuā ḍābar dharti haikh? Mag.

def: v. n. To fill with tears; to be ready to shed tears. 2. Itnī sī ḍibyā ḍab ḍab kare, māṅak motī jhaṛ jhaṛ paṛe . Wom. Riddle. Ans. Āṅkh . Eye. A tiny box, now open, now shut close, A string of pearls so bright now from it flows.

ety: dab˚ = Prk. दम्म(इ) or दव्व(इ)? = S. दम्य(ते), pass. fr. rt. दम्
def: v.n. To be pressed down, be compressed; to be depressed, be bowed down; to be squeezed, be crushed, be buried (under); to sink, to fall; to bend (under pressure or weight); — to be concealed, to lie hid, to lurk, to crouch; — to be restrained, be checked, be repressed, be suppressed, be quelled; to be subdued, be tamed, be awed, be cowed, be snubbed; to submit to control or authority, to yield (to force, &c.); to cower; to shrink; — to be kept back, be withheld; to be kept down, be retrenched; — to fall short (in measure, &c.)
more: v. n. S. दमन् 1. To fall; sink; go down. Miṭṭī dab gaī . 2. To be buried, interred ( dafan h .). 3. To be hidden, concealed. 4. To be quelled or put down (insurrection, sedition). 1. Jhagṛā dab gayā . 2. Bāt dab gaī . 5. To be kept back, withheld, retrenched, lost to one. 6. To be checked, snubbed, kept down, restrained, suppressed. 7. To be pressed, squeezed, crushed. 1. Hāth dab gayā . 2. Kunbe ke kharch meṅ dab gayā . 8. To be shampoed, kneaded. Pāṅoṅ dabnā . 9. To yield to authority or force; sink under; come under one's power; to bow to; to be overcome, overawed; to turn tail. 1. Us se sab dabte haiṅ . 2. Ab bachchā bahut dab gayā . 10. To fall short or be under the full measure, etc. dab marnā , v. n. To be crushed to death. dabī billī , n. f. A cowed cat. dabe pāṅoṅ , adv. With silent steps; softly; gently. 1. Yeh kah nīche utrā dabe pāoṅ woh, Nazar se bachāë huë chhāoṅ woh . Mīr …

v. n. 1. To be snubbed, to crouch, shrink, be pressed down, be bowed down, give way, to be awed. 2. To be concealed. دب آنا dab-ānā, v. n. To advance. دب جانا dab jānā, To retire, to withdraw, to be mortified, to be suppressed. دب چلنا يا نکلنا dab-chalnā or -nikalnā, To be awed, to be overpowered. دب مرنا dab marnā, To be crushed to death. دبي پانو dabe-pāṅw or دبي پاون dabe-pāoṅ, With silent steps, softly, gently.
subs: v.n. (intens.), To be pressed down; to sink down; to crouch down; to be suppressed, &c. ( = dabnā); to retire, to withdraw
v.n. To be overcome, be subdued; to be awed
v.n. To be crushed; to be suppressed, to be squashed (as a proposal, &c.)
v.n. To be crushed to death

n. f. P. dādan , S. दा to give. 1. ( In comp .) Given; bestowed. Khudā-dād . Given by God. 2. Praise; commendation; applause. 3. Justice; equity. dād chāhnā , v. n. To demand or sue for justice or redress. dād-khuāh , n. m. 1. A petitioner for justice; a suitor; applicant; claimant. 2. ( dādī faryādī ) A complainant; plaintiff; prosecutor. dād-khuāhī , n. f. Application for justice or redress. dād dahish , n. f. Bounty; charity; beneficence. dād-dihī , n. f. Administering justice (داد 3.). dād denā , v. n. 1. To deal out or dispense justice. 2. To appreciate duly; to do justice to. dād-rasī , n. f. Redress; redress of grievances; deliverance from wrong. dād sitad ; H. len den , n. lit . giving and taking. See بيوﮩـار 1 and kharīd farokht . (Sans. दद्रु ) A ringworm. داد بيداد کرنا dād be-dād karnā, To demand justice. داد خواه dād-ḵẖẉāh, Demanding justice, a petitioner for justice, a plaintiff, suitor. داد خواهش dād-ḵẖẉāhish, or داد خواهي dād-ḵẖẉāh-ī, s. f. To make reparation. داد رس dād-ras, Administering justice, redresser of grievances. داد رسي dād-ras-ī, s. f. Redress of grievances, the administering justice. داد کرنا dād karnā, To make complaint (of injustice, &c.). داد کوپـﮩنچنا dād ko pahunchnā, To administer justice, to do justice, to obtain justice. داد گر dād-gar, Administrator…

To demand justice, to seek redress

dād-ḵẖẉāhish, s.f. The demanding of justice, application for redress

adj. Administering justice, just; — one who administers justice, a redresser of grievances
s.f. The administering of justice; redress of grievances, redress, deliverance from oppression or wrong-doing
s.f. Cry for help or redress, application for justice
(-kī), To administer justice, do justice (to); to obtain justice or redress; — to respond to cries for help (from), to come to the aid or rescue (of)
adj. & s.m. Just (prince, &c.); — administrator of justice

To obtain redress, &c. ( = dād pānā; dād-ko pahuṅćnā)

def: s.m. A paternal grandfather; — an elder brother; a term of veneration or respect addressed to Brāhmans by Jāṭs and Gūjars, &c.
more: n. m. S. तात तात 1. (Sant. tātāt ) Paternal grandfather. 2. (Sant. dada ) An elder brother. 3. An appellation of Brahmins, used by Jāṭs and Gūjars . Dāde rāj na khāë pān, dāṅt nipoṛe, gaë pirān . Prov. No pān to eat his grandsire could afford, He could but grin, and so his days out poured.
s. m. 1. Paternal grandfather. 2. Elder brother.
wety: Inherited from Middle Hindi دادا (dada /⁠dādā⁠/) (c. 1564), from Proto-New Indo-Aryan *𑀤𑀸𑀤𑁆𑀤 (*dādda) + Middle Indo-Aryan -𑀓- (-ka-), an onomatopoeic nursery reduplication.

ety: perf. part. of dādan; and = Zend dāta, rt. dā; S. datta (Vedic dāta), rt. dā 'to give'
def: part. Given, bestowed, imparted; — having given (used chiefly in comp., e.g. tāb-dāda, 'heat-imparted,' inflamed).
more: part. (of دادن) Given, having given. (Pers. plur. دادگان).

def: s.f. A female servant, a nurse (particularly an old nurse that has attended upon one from her youth); a maid-servant.
more: n. f. A maid servant; nurse.
(Pers. دادا or Turk. دده) s. f. A maidservant, a nurse.

def: s.m. = dhadhaććhar, q.v. s.m. lit. 'Burnt letters'; in versification, certain words or letters which are considered by Hindī poets to be unlucky (as ह, ग, न at the beginning of a verse; र, ज, स in the middle; and क, ट, ज्ञ at the end).
v.n. = dhadhaknā, q.v. v.n. To burn fiercely, to blaze, to flare.
s.f. Advances (of money, pay, &c., made to cultivators, labourers, manufacturers, &c.); — debts
more: n. f. 1. Advances for purchase of produce; advancing pay to laborers and manufacturers 2. Debts. dādnī-dār , Ped. n. m. One who has taken advances on a contract. nīl dādnī , n. f. An advance made for the cultivation of indigo.
subs: s.m. One who has received advances of money.

ety: Old P. dādhār; Pehl. dātār; Zend dātar, rt. dā; S. धातर, rt. धा
def: s.m. The Creator, God.
more: (داد Justice, and part. act. of آوردن To bring) s. m. Bringer or distributer of justice (an epithet of the deity).

more: Bhoj. n. f. The mother-in-law of the wife's or husband's mother-in-law.

more: Mah. n. m. dādā grand father, susrā father-in-law. The father-in-law of the wife's or husband's father-in-law.

s.m. The churning of coagulated milk.

def: n. m. S. दधि Coagulated milk; tyre ( dahī ). E rī, bīr, guālan, Gopāl kahūṅ dekho āj? Khāë dadh, mākhan churāë-kar, hirāno rī, Kaheṅ Padmākar yūṅ, sun Jasodhā ke bain, Boli bol guālan, piyūk-ras sāno rī, 'Terī soṅ ri Nand-rānī, det hūṅ batāë toë, Tero so thag Brij meṅ na lakhāno rī, Gopin ko chor, mero chit-chor, tero mākhan ko chor, Merī āṅkhan samāno rī .' Brij. Kabit. Padmākar Where, sister milkmaid, is Gupāl, have you seen him to-day? He milk and butter stole and straightway slily slunk away, Padmākar heard Jasodhā and in accents nectar-sweet, The milkmaid said 'I tell you in all Brij there's not a cheat To match your son, the milkmaids' thief, my heart's, your butter's too, Within my eyes know then your son's dear image you may view!' dadh kādo , n. m. A festival in honor of Krishn.
more: s. m. Sour, thick milk, coagulated milk. دده کادو dadhi-kādo or دده کادون dadhi-kādauṅ ( दधिकर्द्दम ) Coagulated milk and clay, thrown by people at each other in sport on the festival of Kr̤ish̤ṇaʼs birthday (بهادون بدي اشٿمي).

s.m. The churning of coagulated milk.

def: s.m. One suing for justice, a plaintiff, complainant

more: s. m. Descent (by ancestors), pedigree, ancestors, paternal grandfatherʼs family.

more: s. m. A bump (occasioned by the bite of an insect).

subs: To proclaim by beat of drum.

more: n. m. 1. A kind of song to a quick air, so called from its resemblance to the quick notes of the frog. This class of songs is most common in Agra and Bundelkand. 2. The palate. Dādrā baiṭh gayā .
s. m. A kind of song.

ety: prob. fr. S. दृ, by redupl.
def: s.f. Unripe corn (chiefly barley) which is cut from time to time and brought home to be eaten without being taken to the threshing-floor.
more: n. f. H. dūdh , milk. Unripe corn, chiefly barley which is cut occasionally and brought home to be eaten without being taken to the threshing-floor.

def: s.f. A cutaneous eruption or rash, herpes, &c. (see dād)
subs: adj. & s.m. Herpetic; — a person having herpes.

def: s.m. Father-in-law of a wife's or husband's father-in-law.
s.m. Name of a famous faqīr
more: n. prop. A famous faqīr, founder of the Dādūpanthī sect. Bate. dādū-panthī , A follower of Dādū .
n. prop. Name of a celebrated faḳīr. دادو پنتهي dādū-panthī, Follower of Dādū.
subs: s.m. Name of the sect who are followers of the faqīr Dādū; a member of that sect.
wety: Named after Syed Dad Muhammad Shah Alias Dadu, the founder of the city.

def: v.n. To low, to bellow (as cattle; = ḍakārne, q.v.); to roar; to thunder.
s.m. A frog.
more: Poet. n. m. S. दर्दूर, Pālī, dadduro ; Panj. ḍiḍḍū (Sant. roṭe ; H. ṭarrū ). A frog ( meṅḍak ). 1. Sakhī, lāgā Sāḍh, ghanghor garje, Ban bole dādur, mor morā jiā larje . Song. Bārahmāsā . Dear friend! August is come, the heavy clouds are thundering, the frog and peacock in the forest cry, my heart goes pit-a-pat. 2. Dādur se kyā kyā kahīye, pīyā bin kaise rahīye, Mujh birhan bin pī, rī sakhī! Song. Ṭhumrī .

more: adj. Perpetual, continual, lasting, durable, standing.

To look right and left; to use one's eyes; to be careful. dāëṅ bāëṅ k. , v. a. To hide; conceal. Are ṭohī lāg rahī saī! ubdū chīj dāṅhṛeṅ bāṅmeṅ kar do! Rus. Dont you see they are searching the houses? Put the stolen articles out of the way.
On the right and left.

more: n. f. The booming of guns.

To look right and left, to keep a sharp look out, to be vigilant or careful
v.t. 'To put to the right and left'; to put out of the way, to stow away, secrete, conceal.

def: E. n. f. S. दा to part. 1. Threshing the corn by bullocks. Some six or eight, in a row, are driven round a central pivot over the corn strewed upon the floor. 2. The unthreshed corn in the ear.
more: adj. Equal in age or size, etc. ( barābar 9.). 1. Dekh! terī dāīṅ kī chhorī kaisā kām kare hai! Wom. See, how that little girl works! she is your own age! 2. Yeh kyā terī dāīṅ kā thā jo tū ne mārā? (Said to a big child who has beaten a little one).
adj. S. दक्षिण On the right; on the right hand side. dāyāṅ bolnā , The cry of a partridge on one's right, considered a bad omen by travellers and thieves.

def: n. m. 1. A circle; ring (of persons); an orbit. 2. A tambourine. 3. A monastery ( khānqāh ). dāëre-dār , The head of a monastery; abbot. dāëra-i-qutbī ; S. ध्रुववृत्त Polar circle. muhīt-i-dāëra ; H. chakkar-gher . Circumference.
more: (from دور) s. m. 1. A circle, a ring, circumference, orbit. 2. A tambourin. 3. A monastery. دايره دار dāʼịra-dār, Chief of a monastery, an abbot, prior, &c.
s. m. A tambourin (the largest variety being from a foot and a half to two feet in diameter, played on with a stick: Qanoon-e-islam ).

def: s.m. = ḍaph, daf, q.v. s.m. A tambourine.
more: s. (dakh.) A kind of tambourin (which consists of a wooden dish or plate, covered with leather, and is about a foot or somewhat less in diameter: Qanoon-e-islam ).

ety: inf. n. of دفع 'to push, to repel,' &c.

more: (Heb. ) 1. s. m. Repulsion, repelling, averting, preventing, warding off. 2. (for دفعه) s. f. A time, a turn. دفعدار dafʼaﻌ-dār, s. m. See دفعه دار under دفعه dafʼa. دفع کرنا dafﻌ karnā, To repel, to ward off, to avert, to remove. دفع هونا dafﻌ honā, To be turned back, to return.
subs: v.t. To push or thrust away or off, to dispel, remove, repel, avert, ward off, parry; to guard (oneself, against, -se)
v.n. To be beaten off, be repelled, &c.; to be turned back; to return.

more: n. m. A large flat drum or tambourine without the brass cymbals.
n. m. 1. Ebullition; overflow excess. 2. Noxious property; bitter; poison. daf marnā , v. n. To be extracted (a noxious property); to be moderated; reduced.
n. m. A kind of drum. See ḍhap .
s. m. A sort of base tambourin played on with a stick ( Qanoon-e-islam ).
subs: (-kā), Disagreeableness (of a thing) to be removed, noxious property to be extracted or destroyed.

vulg. dafʻe-dar, s.m. An officer commanding a small body of cavalry or infantry
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian دَفْعَه (daf'a), from Arabic دَفْعَة (dafʕa).

vulg. dafʻe-dar, s.m. An officer commanding a small body of cavalry or infantry
